text_blocks.text: Process-level concurrency with fork() is powerful, but inflexible.
The OS schedules the task, incurring context switching overhead.

runs.text: The OS schedules the task, incurring context switching overhead.

text_blocks.text: The process memory is copied making it hard to share data among tasks.

runs.text: making it hard to share data among tasks.

text_blocks.text: A thread is a concurrency primitive that is inner-process.

runs.text: is a concurrency primitive that is inner-process.

text_blocks.text: The program itself schedules the task as part of the same process.

runs.text: The program itself schedules the task as part of the same process.

text_blocks.text: Process memory, therefore, is shared across all threads.

runs.text: Process memory, therefore, is shared across all threads.

text_blocks.text: The 2011 amendment to the C standard (C11) added a threading API.

runs.text: The 2011 amendment to the C standard (C11) added a threading API.

text_blocks.text: We will be reviewing the pthread standard.
The C11 threads.h API is still very similar.
There are ports of the pthread.h interface to many OSes.
Lots of threading APIs in other language emulate it.

runs.text: Lots of threading APIs in other language emulate it.

text_blocks.text: The “p” in pthread stands for the Portable Operating System Interface (POSIX).

runs.text: stands for the Portable Operating System Interface (

text_blocks.text: This is a standard for creating OS abstractions.

runs.text: This is a standard for creating OS abstractions.

text_blocks.text: Intended to lower the burden of porting applications.

runs.text: Intended to lower the burden of porting applications.

text_blocks.text: Most OSes conform to most POSIX standards.

runs.text: Most OSes conform to most POSIX standards.

text_blocks.text: However, very few OSes fully implement POSIX.

runs.text: However, very few OSes fully implement POSIX.

text_blocks.text: POSIX standardizes threads, but also process creation and the behavior of fork, file abstractions, and how data is shared among processes.

runs.text: POSIX standardizes threads, but also process creation and the behavior of fork, file abstractions, and how data is shared among processes.

text_blocks.text: Many OS interfaces are POSIX interfaces and remain (mostly) true across different platforms.

runs.text: Many OS interfaces are POSIX interfaces and remain (mostly) true across different platforms.
